content:
# Benchmark: depth-max-interval=1 grading — clips 0055 and 0057 (2026-04-04)

**Date:** 2026-04-04
**Pipeline version:** unified keyframe sampling (post-PR)
**Flag:** `--depth-max-interval 1`

## Results

| Clip | Frames | Keyframes | Wall time | Overall fps |
|------|--------|-----------|-----------|-------------|
| 0055 (14.0s) | 1,671 | 836 (50%) | **5m 1.824s** | 5.5 |
| 0057 (41.3s) | 4,943 | 2,500 (50%) | **14m 29.115s** | 5.7 |

## Stage breakdown — clip 0055

| Stage | Time |
|-------|------|
| Pass 1 (836 keyframes detected) | 14s |
| Fused inference (836 keyframes) | ~2m 4s |
| Grading pass (1671 frames) | ~2m 33s |

## Stage breakdown — clip 0057

| Stage | Time |
|-------|------|
| Pass 1 (2500 keyframes detected) | 40s |
| Fused inference (2500 keyframes) | ~6m 7s |
| Grading pass (4943 frames) | ~7m 4s |

## Key findings

**~50% keyframe rate at interval=1 on 120fps footage.** Both clips yield exactly ~50% keyframes (836/1671, 2500/4943) despite `--depth-max-interval 1`. The MSE skip threshold still filters roughly half of consecutive 120fps frames as visually redundant — consecutive frames are too similar to exceed the threshold, so the max-interval safety valve only fires every other frame.

**Grading pass is unaffected by keyframe density.** Grading pass for 0057 is ~7m 4s at interval=1 vs ~7m 9s at default — essentially identical. All extra cost is in pre-grading depth inference. This confirms the batch-then-grade architecture decouples depth density from grading throughput.

**Throughput scales linearly with frame count.** 5.5–5.7 fps overall across both clips (3× difference in length) confirms predictable scaling.

## Cost of interval=1 vs default

| Clip | Default wall time | interval=1 wall time | Extra cost |
|------|-------------------|---------------------|------------|
| 0055 | ~2m 6.9s (combined-LUT baseline) | 5m 1.8s | +2m 55s (+138%) |
| 0057 | 12m 12.3s (unified-keyframe) | 14m 29.1s | +2m 16.8s (+18%) |

The relative overhead is larger on the shorter clip (0055) because fixed startup/calibration costs dominate more at 1,671 frames.
